channel.title: Tesla Shorts Time Daily
channel.description: A daily podcast covering the latest Tesla news, stock prices, and industry insights.
channel.itunes:author: Patrick
channel.itunes:summary: A daily podcast covering the latest Tesla news, stock prices, and industry insights.

item.title: Ep 395: Tesla's FSD (Supervised) fleet hits 8.4 billion cumulative miles, closing in on the 10 billion needed for unsupervised autonomy.
item.description:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** March 01, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $402.51 ▼ $4.27 (1.0%)
**HOOK:** Tesla's FSD (Supervised) fleet hits 8.4 billion cumulative miles, closing in on the 10 billion needed for unsupervised autonomy.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Tesla FSD (Supervised) fleet passes 8.4 billion cumulative miles: 28 February, 2026, 11:57 PM PST, Teslarati** Tesla's Full Self-Driving (Supervised) system has now exceeded 8.4 billion miles driven, as updated on the company's safety page, highlighting the rapid accumulation of real-world data for neural network training. This milestone matters for Tesla's autonomy ambitions, as it approaches Elon Musk's estimated 10 billion mile threshold to handle rare edge cases effectively, potentially accelerating regulatory approval for unsupervised driving. For the industry, it underscores how data volume drives AI advancements in self-driving tech, giving Tesla a competitive edge over rivals with smaller datasets. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/tesla-fsd-supervised-fleet-passes-8-4-billion-cumulative-miles/
2. **Tesla expands global FSD (Supervised) testing with Abu Dhabi trials: 28 February, 2026, 9:49 PM PST, Teslarati** Tesla has launched its first formal testing program for Full Self-Driving (Supervised) in Abu Dhabi, marking a key step in global expansion of the technology. This development is significant for Tesla's business as it opens new markets in the Middle East, where regulatory frameworks are adapting to autonomous tech, potentially boosting adoption and sales. For customers, it means more diverse real-world data to refine FSD, improving reliability in varied driving conditions like desert environments. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/tesla-global-fsd-testing-abu-dhabi/
3. **Tesla Rebrands Autopilot Features in New 2026.2.9 Software Update: 28 February, 2026, 9:18 PM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Tesla's latest software update renames "Navigate on Autopilot" to "Navigate on Autosteer" and updates hardware references like the FSD Computer, signaling a shift in branding for driver-assistance features. This matters for the technology as it clarifies supervised autonomy to users and regulators, reducing confusion amid scrutiny over self-driving claims. For customers, the rebranding could improve user experience by aligning terminology with actual capabilities, while hinting at future unsupervised evolutions. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/02/28/tesla-rebrands-autopilot-features-in-new-2026-2-9-software-update/
4. **Tesla Shares New Battery Care Tips, Warns Against Relying on Superchargers Daily: 28 February, 2026, 1:32 PM PST, Drive Tesla** Tesla updated the Model Y owner's manual with battery maintenance advice, emphasizing home charging over daily Supercharger use to preserve long-term battery health. This is important for customers as it provides practical guidance to maximize vehicle longevity and performance, addressing common concerns about degradation. For Tesla's business, it reinforces the ecosystem of home charging solutions, encouraging Wall Connector sales amid growing EV adoption. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/tesla-shares-new-battery-care-tips-warns-against-relying-on-superchargers-daily/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-shares-new-battery-care-tips-warns-against-relying-on-superchargers-daily
5. **Tesla Vehicles Finally Come To Africa: 28 February, 2026, 7:30 AM PST, CleanTechnica** Tesla announced that Model 3 and Model Y will be available in Morocco starting mid-2026, marking the company's first entry into an African market. This expansion matters for Tesla's international growth, tapping into emerging EV demand in regions with solar potential and infrastructure needs. For the industry, it signals broader global access to electric vehicles, potentially accelerating clean energy transitions in developing markets. Source: https://cleantechnica.com/2026/02/28/tesla-vehicles-finally-come-...
item.itunes:summary:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** March 01, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $402.51 ▼ $4.27 (1.0%)
**HOOK:** Tesla's FSD (Supervised) fleet hits 8.4 billion cumulative miles, closing in on the 10 billion needed for unsupervised autonomy.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Tesla FSD (Supervised) fleet passes 8.4 billion cumulative miles: 28 February, 2026, 11:57 PM PST, Teslarati** Tesla's Full Self-Driving (Supervised) system has now exceeded 8.4 billion miles driven, as updated on the company's safety page, highlighting the rapid accumulation of real-world data for neural network training. This milestone matters for Tesla's autonomy ambitions, as it approaches Elon Musk's estimated 10 billion mile threshold to handle rare edge cases effectively, potentially accelerating regulatory approval for unsupervised driving. For the industry, it underscores how data volume drives AI advancements in self-driving tech, giving Tesla a competitive edge over rivals with smaller datasets. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/tesla-fsd-supervised-fleet-passes-8-4-billion-cumulative-miles/
2. **Tesla expands global FSD (Supervised) testing with Abu Dhabi trials: 28 February, 2026, 9:49 PM PST, Teslarati** Tesla has launched its first formal testing program for Full Self-Driving (Supervised) in Abu Dhabi, marking a key step in global expansion of the technology. This development is significant for Tesla's business as it opens new markets in the Middle East, where regulatory frameworks are adapting to autonomous tech, potentially boosting adoption and sales. For customers, it means more diverse real-world data to refine FSD, improving reliability in varied driving conditions like desert environments. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/tesla-global-fsd-testing-abu-dhabi/
3. **Tesla Rebrands Autopilot Features in New 2026.2.9 Software Update: 28 February, 2026, 9:18 PM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Tesla's latest software update renames "Navigate on Autopilot" to "Navigate on Autosteer" and updates hardware references like the FSD Computer, signaling a shift in branding for driver-assistance features. This matters for the technology as it clarifies supervised autonomy to users and regulators, reducing confusion amid scrutiny over self-driving claims. For customers, the rebranding could improve user experience by aligning terminology with actual capabilities, while hinting at future unsupervised evolutions. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/02/28/tesla-rebrands-autopilot-features-in-new-2026-2-9-software-update/
4. **Tesla Shares New Battery Care Tips, Warns Against Relying on Superchargers Daily: 28 February, 2026, 1:32 PM PST, Drive Tesla** Tesla updated the Model Y owner's manual with battery maintenance advice, emphasizing home charging over daily Supercharger use to preserve long-term battery health. This is important for customers as it provides practical guidance to maximize vehicle longevity and performance, addressing common concerns about degradation. For Tesla's business, it reinforces the ecosystem of home charging solutions, encouraging Wall Connector sales amid growing EV adoption. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/tesla-shares-new-battery-care-tips-warns-against-relying-on-superchargers-daily/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-shares-new-battery-care-tips-warns-against-relying-on-superchargers-daily
5. **Tesla Vehicles Finally Come To Africa: 28 February, 2026, 7:30 AM PST, CleanTechnica** Tesla announced that Model 3 and Model Y will be available in Morocco starting mid-2026, marking the company's first entry into an African market. This expansion matters for Tesla's international growth, tapping into emerging EV demand in regions with solar potential and infrastructure needs. For the industry, it signals broader global access to electric vehicles, potentially accelerating clean energy transitions in developing markets. Source: https://cleantechnica.com/2026/02/28/tesla-vehicles-finally-come-...
item.itunes:title: Ep 395: Tesla's FSD (Supervised) fleet hits 8.4 billion cumulative miles, closing in on the 10 billion needed for unsupervised autonomy.

item.title: Ep 393: Tesla's new affordable Cybertruck variant sees delivery estimates pushed to April 2027 amid surging demand.
item.description:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** February 27, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $399.50 ▼ $7.28 (1.8%)
**HOOK:** Tesla's new affordable Cybertruck variant sees delivery estimates pushed to April 2027 amid surging demand.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Tesla Model Y 7-Seater Returns to Europe on Premium AWD: 27 February, 2026, 10:09 AM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Tesla has reintroduced the seven-seat option for the Model Y in Europe, limited to the Premium Long Range All-Wheel Drive model, allowing customers in countries like Germany and France to add third-row jump seats. This move addresses strong demand for family-oriented EVs in the region, potentially boosting Tesla's sales by catering to larger households and competing more directly with rivals offering versatile seating. For Tesla's business, it strengthens its position in Europe's growing EV market where practicality drives adoption. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/02/27/tesla-model-y-7-seater-returns-to-europe-on-premium-awd/
2. **Tesla Model Y Canada Update: 16-Inch Screen & Black Headliner Arrive: 27 February, 2026, 10:03 AM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Tesla updated its Canadian Model Y configurator to include a standard black headliner and a larger 16-inch QHD rear screen on Premium and Performance trims. These enhancements improve interior aesthetics and passenger experience, making the vehicle more appealing in a competitive market where cabin features influence buyer decisions. This could help Tesla maintain its lead in Canada by aligning with customer feedback on premium touches. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/02/27/tesla-model-y-canada-update-16-inch-screen-black-headliner-arrive/
3. **Tesla Lands Major Powerwall Distribution Deal in Australia: 27 February, 2026, 9:50 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Tesla secured a nationwide distribution agreement with Supply Partners Group for its Powerwall home battery system in Australia, building on its already strong popularity there. This partnership expands access to energy storage solutions, which matters for Tesla's energy business by accelerating adoption in a market prone to grid instability and high solar penetration. It positions Tesla to capture more of the residential energy market amid growing demand for reliable backups. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/tesla-lands-major-powerwall-distribution-deal-in-australia/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-lands-major-powerwall-distribution-deal-in-australia
4. **Tesla drops latest hint that new Cybertruck trim is selling like hotcakes: 27 February, 2026, 9:41 AM PST, Teslarati** Tesla's online design studio now shows April 2027 delivery for new All-Wheel-Drive Cybertruck orders, indicating high demand for the more affordable variant. This reflects strong consumer interest in Tesla's electric truck lineup, which could drive revenue growth but also highlights production scaling challenges. For the industry, it underscores the appeal of accessible EV trucks in shifting transportation paradigms. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/tesla-new-cybertruck-delivery-date-pushed-back-once-again/
5. **Virgin Media O2 Launches Europe’s First Starlink Direct-to-Cell Service in the UK: 27 February, 2026, 9:30 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Virgin Media O2 activated Europe's first commercial Starlink direct-to-cell service, branded O2 Satellite, providing satellite-powered mobile data to UK customers. While tied to SpaceX, this advances Tesla-adjacent tech in connectivity, potentially benefiting future integrations like in-vehicle systems. It matters for the industry by expanding remote access, which could influence EV ecosystems reliant on constant data links. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/spacex/virgin-media-o2-launches-europes-first-starlink-direct-to-cell-service-in-the-uk/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=virgin-media-o2-launches-europes-first-starlink-direct-to-cell-service-in-the-uk
6. **Tesla extends financing offers in China for ...
item.itunes:summary:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** February 27, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $399.50 ▼ $7.28 (1.8%)
**HOOK:** Tesla's new affordable Cybertruck variant sees delivery estimates pushed to April 2027 amid surging demand.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Tesla Model Y 7-Seater Returns to Europe on Premium AWD: 27 February, 2026, 10:09 AM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Tesla has reintroduced the seven-seat option for the Model Y in Europe, limited to the Premium Long Range All-Wheel Drive model, allowing customers in countries like Germany and France to add third-row jump seats. This move addresses strong demand for family-oriented EVs in the region, potentially boosting Tesla's sales by catering to larger households and competing more directly with rivals offering versatile seating. For Tesla's business, it strengthens its position in Europe's growing EV market where practicality drives adoption. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/02/27/tesla-model-y-7-seater-returns-to-europe-on-premium-awd/
2. **Tesla Model Y Canada Update: 16-Inch Screen & Black Headliner Arrive: 27 February, 2026, 10:03 AM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Tesla updated its Canadian Model Y configurator to include a standard black headliner and a larger 16-inch QHD rear screen on Premium and Performance trims. These enhancements improve interior aesthetics and passenger experience, making the vehicle more appealing in a competitive market where cabin features influence buyer decisions. This could help Tesla maintain its lead in Canada by aligning with customer feedback on premium touches. Source: https://teslanorth.com/2026/02/27/tesla-model-y-canada-update-16-inch-screen-black-headliner-arrive/
3. **Tesla Lands Major Powerwall Distribution Deal in Australia: 27 February, 2026, 9:50 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Tesla secured a nationwide distribution agreement with Supply Partners Group for its Powerwall home battery system in Australia, building on its already strong popularity there. This partnership expands access to energy storage solutions, which matters for Tesla's energy business by accelerating adoption in a market prone to grid instability and high solar penetration. It positions Tesla to capture more of the residential energy market amid growing demand for reliable backups. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/tesla-lands-major-powerwall-distribution-deal-in-australia/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-lands-major-powerwall-distribution-deal-in-australia
4. **Tesla drops latest hint that new Cybertruck trim is selling like hotcakes: 27 February, 2026, 9:41 AM PST, Teslarati** Tesla's online design studio now shows April 2027 delivery for new All-Wheel-Drive Cybertruck orders, indicating high demand for the more affordable variant. This reflects strong consumer interest in Tesla's electric truck lineup, which could drive revenue growth but also highlights production scaling challenges. For the industry, it underscores the appeal of accessible EV trucks in shifting transportation paradigms. Source: https://www.teslarati.com/tesla-new-cybertruck-delivery-date-pushed-back-once-again/
5. **Virgin Media O2 Launches Europe’s First Starlink Direct-to-Cell Service in the UK: 27 February, 2026, 9:30 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Virgin Media O2 activated Europe's first commercial Starlink direct-to-cell service, branded O2 Satellite, providing satellite-powered mobile data to UK customers. While tied to SpaceX, this advances Tesla-adjacent tech in connectivity, potentially benefiting future integrations like in-vehicle systems. It matters for the industry by expanding remote access, which could influence EV ecosystems reliant on constant data links. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/spacex/virgin-media-o2-launches-europes-first-starlink-direct-to-cell-service-in-the-uk/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=virgin-media-o2-launches-europes-first-starlink-direct-to-cell-service-in-the-uk
6. **Tesla extends financing offers in China for ...
item.itunes:title: Ep 393: Tesla's new affordable Cybertruck variant sees delivery estimates pushed to April 2027 amid surging demand.

item.title: Ep 392: Tesla warns of potential Giga Berlin expansion delays amid union tensions, just days before key works council elections.
item.description:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** February 26, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $408.18 ▼ $7.01 (1.7%)
**HOOK:** Tesla warns of potential Giga Berlin expansion delays amid union tensions, just days before key works council elections.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Tesla leads as Canada adds nearly 2,000 DC fast charging ports in 2025: 26 February, 2026, 11:10 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Canada expanded its DC fast-charging network by nearly 2,000 ports in 2025, with Tesla leading utili...

item.description: Daily Tesla news digest for January 06, 2026 at 03:12 AM PST. TSLA price: $451.67 +0.56 (+0.12%) . 1️⃣ **Tesla Reports 418,227 Deliveries for the Fourth Quarter, Down 16%: 02 January, 2026, 09:00 AM PST, CNBC**...

item.title: Ep 391: Tesla's Cybertruck Dual Motor AWD delivery estimates have slipped to early fall 2026 due to high demand.
item.description:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** February 26, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $417.40 ▲ $2.28 (0.5%)
**HOOK:** Tesla's Cybertruck Dual Motor AWD delivery estimates have slipped to early fall 2026 due to high demand.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Tesla Reportedly Bringing New HD Screen and Dark Cabin to Model 3: 25 February, 2026, 1:51 PM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Tesla is set to upgrade the Model 3 with a new HD screen and dark cabin interior, aligning it with refreshes in other...
item.itunes:summary:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** February 26, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $417.40 ▲ $2.28 (0.5%)
**HOOK:** Tesla's Cybertruck Dual Motor AWD delivery estimates have slipped to early fall 2026 due to high demand.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Tesla Reportedly Bringing New HD Screen and Dark Cabin to Model 3: 25 February, 2026, 1:51 PM PST, TeslaNorth.com** Tesla is set to upgrade the Model 3 with a new HD screen and dark cabin interior, aligning it with refreshes in other...
item.itunes:title: Ep 391: Tesla's Cybertruck Dual Motor AWD delivery estimates have slipped to early fall 2026 due to high demand.

item.title: Ep 394: Tesla confirms upgraded black headliner and 16-inch screen for new Model Y orders in Canada, enhancing customer appeal.
item.description:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** February 28, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $402.51 ▼ $4.27 (1.0%)
**HOOK:** Tesla confirms upgraded black headliner and 16-inch screen for new Model Y orders in Canada, enhancing customer appeal.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Rivian Launches RAD: A New Performance Division to Push Its EVs Even Further: 27 February, 2026, 10:23 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Rivian has officially launched its Rivian Adventure Department (RAD), an in-house team focused on boosting the performance of its electric vehicles through innovative engineering. This move signals Rivian's push to compete more aggressively in the high-performance EV segment, potentially challenging Tesla's dominance in areas like off-road and adventure-focused models. For the industry, it highlights the growing emphasis on specialized divisions to accelerate EV innovation, which could pressure Tesla to continue evolving its Cybertruck and other rugged offerings. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/rivian-launches-rad-a-new-performance-division-to-push-its-evs-even-further/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rivian-launches-rad-a-new-performance-division-to-push-its-evs-even-further
2. **High Above the Clouds: How Long-Haul Flights Are Becoming Truly Connected for French Travelers: 27 February, 2026, 10:05 AM PST, WhatsUpTesla** Air France has rolled out free high-speed Wi-Fi on long-haul flights to destinations like Guadeloupe and Martinique, powered by SpaceX's Starlink technology. This development showcases how Starlink is transforming connectivity in aviation, making flights more productive for passengers and crediting Elon Musk's team for the advancement. For Tesla's broader ecosystem, it underscores the synergies between SpaceX and Tesla in advancing tech infrastructure, potentially boosting investor confidence in Musk-led ventures. Source: https://whatsuptesla.com/2026/02/27/high-above-the-clouds-how-long-haul-flights-are-becoming-truly-connected-for-french-travelers/
3. **Tesla confirms black headliner and 16” screen for new Canadian Model Y orders: 27 February, 2026, 7:13 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Tesla has updated its Canadian Model Y configurator to include a black headliner and a larger 16-inch rear screen, features that were previously unavailable in the region. This upgrade improves the interior experience for customers, aligning Canadian options with those in other markets and likely boosting sales amid competitive pressures. From a business perspective, it demonstrates Tesla's responsiveness to regional demands, helping maintain market share in Canada's growing EV sector. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/model-y/tesla-confirms-black-headliner-and-16-screen-for-new-canadian-model-y-orders/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-confirms-black-headliner-and-16-screen-for-new-canadian-model-y-orders
4. **Internal Records Show Government Knew iZEV Was Nearing Empty Before Tesla’s $43M Claims: 27 February, 2026, 6:24 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Internal documents reveal that Transport Canada knew the iZEV rebate program was running low on funds as early as November 2024, yet failed to notify the public before Tesla submitted $43 million in claims. This oversight could affect Tesla's reimbursement and highlights regulatory hurdles in EV incentives, potentially impacting sales strategies in Canada. For the industry, it raises questions about the reliability of government subsidies, pushing companies like Tesla to advocate for more stable support mechanisms. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/internal-records-show-government-knew-izev-was-nearing-empty-before-teslas-43m-claims/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=internal-records-show-government-knew-izev-was-nearing-empty-before-teslas-43m-claims
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ## Tesla X Takeover: What's Hot Right Now 🎙️ Tesla X Takeover - What's breaking in the Tesla world today! Here are the most interesting, fresh Tesla deve...
item.itunes:summary:
# Tesla Shorts Time **Date:** February 28, 2026 **REAL-TIME TSLA price:** $402.51 ▼ $4.27 (1.0%)
**HOOK:** Tesla confirms upgraded black headliner and 16-inch screen for new Model Y orders in Canada, enhancing customer appeal.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ### Top 10 News Items 1. **Rivian Launches RAD: A New Performance Division to Push Its EVs Even Further: 27 February, 2026, 10:23 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Rivian has officially launched its Rivian Adventure Department (RAD), an in-house team focused on boosting the performance of its electric vehicles through innovative engineering. This move signals Rivian's push to compete more aggressively in the high-performance EV segment, potentially challenging Tesla's dominance in areas like off-road and adventure-focused models. For the industry, it highlights the growing emphasis on specialized divisions to accelerate EV innovation, which could pressure Tesla to continue evolving its Cybertruck and other rugged offerings. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/rivian-launches-rad-a-new-performance-division-to-push-its-evs-even-further/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rivian-launches-rad-a-new-performance-division-to-push-its-evs-even-further
2. **High Above the Clouds: How Long-Haul Flights Are Becoming Truly Connected for French Travelers: 27 February, 2026, 10:05 AM PST, WhatsUpTesla** Air France has rolled out free high-speed Wi-Fi on long-haul flights to destinations like Guadeloupe and Martinique, powered by SpaceX's Starlink technology. This development showcases how Starlink is transforming connectivity in aviation, making flights more productive for passengers and crediting Elon Musk's team for the advancement. For Tesla's broader ecosystem, it underscores the synergies between SpaceX and Tesla in advancing tech infrastructure, potentially boosting investor confidence in Musk-led ventures. Source: https://whatsuptesla.com/2026/02/27/high-above-the-clouds-how-long-haul-flights-are-becoming-truly-connected-for-french-travelers/
3. **Tesla confirms black headliner and 16” screen for new Canadian Model Y orders: 27 February, 2026, 7:13 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Tesla has updated its Canadian Model Y configurator to include a black headliner and a larger 16-inch rear screen, features that were previously unavailable in the region. This upgrade improves the interior experience for customers, aligning Canadian options with those in other markets and likely boosting sales amid competitive pressures. From a business perspective, it demonstrates Tesla's responsiveness to regional demands, helping maintain market share in Canada's growing EV sector. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/model-y/tesla-confirms-black-headliner-and-16-screen-for-new-canadian-model-y-orders/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=tesla-confirms-black-headliner-and-16-screen-for-new-canadian-model-y-orders
4. **Internal Records Show Government Knew iZEV Was Nearing Empty Before Tesla’s $43M Claims: 27 February, 2026, 6:24 AM PST, Drive Tesla** Internal documents reveal that Transport Canada knew the iZEV rebate program was running low on funds as early as November 2024, yet failed to notify the public before Tesla submitted $43 million in claims. This oversight could affect Tesla's reimbursement and highlights regulatory hurdles in EV incentives, potentially impacting sales strategies in Canada. For the industry, it raises questions about the reliability of government subsidies, pushing companies like Tesla to advocate for more stable support mechanisms. Source: https://driveteslacanada.ca/news/internal-records-show-government-knew-izev-was-nearing-empty-before-teslas-43m-claims/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=internal-records-show-government-knew-izev-was-nearing-empty-before-teslas-43m-claims
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ## Tesla X Takeover: What's Hot Right Now 🎙️ Tesla X Takeover - What's breaking in the Tesla world today! Here are the most interesting, fresh Tesla deve...
item.itunes:title: Ep 394: Tesla confirms upgraded black headliner and 16-inch screen for new Model Y orders in Canada, enhancing customer appeal.
